questions.text: A car accelerates from rest to 20 m/s in 5 seconds. What is its acceleration?
questions.explanation: Acceleration a = (v - u) / t = (20 - 0) / 5 = 4 m/s^2. Using the equation a = delta_v / delta_t.

questions.text: What is the SI unit of electric resistance?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of electrical resistance is the Ohm (symbol: omega), named after Georg Simon Ohm. It is defined as 1 V/A (one volt per ampere).

questions.text: A body of mass 5 kg is lifted to a height of 10 m. What is its gravitational potential energy? (g = 10 m/s^2)
questions.explanation: Gravitational PE = mgh = 5 x 10 x 10 = 500 J. The formula uses mass (m), gravitational field strength (g), and height (h).

questions.text: Which of the following best describes the relationship stated by Ohm's Law?
options: A) Voltage is inversely proportional to current at constant resistance
B) Current is directly proportional to voltage at constant resistance
C) Resistance increases as voltage increases
D) Power equals current squared times voltage
questions.explanation: Ohm's Law: V = IR. This means I = V/R. At constant resistance, current (I) is directly proportional to voltage (V). Doubling V doubles I.

questions.text: The speed of light in a vacuum is approximately:
questions.explanation: The speed of light in a vacuum (c) is approximately 3 x 10^8 m/s (exactly 299,792,458 m/s). This is a fundamental physical constant.

questions.text: A wave has a frequency of 500 Hz and a wavelength of 0.68 m. What is its speed?
questions.explanation: Wave speed v = frequency x wavelength = 500 x 0.68 = 340 m/s. This is approximately the speed of sound in air at room temperature.

options: A) Force equals mass times acceleration
B) An object at rest stays at rest unless acted upon by a net force
C) For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction
D) The net force on an object equals the rate of change of momentum
questions.explanation: Newton's Third Law: For every action (force) there is an equal and opposite reaction force. Example: a rocket expels gas downward; the gas pushes the rocket upward.

questions.text: Which type of electromagnetic radiation has the shortest wavelength?
questions.explanation: Gamma rays have the shortest wavelength (less than 10^-11 m) and highest frequency in the electromagnetic spectrum. Shorter wavelength means higher energy.

questions.text: What happens to the pressure of a fixed amount of gas if its volume is halved at constant temperature?
questions.explanation: By Boyle's Law: P1V1 = P2V2 at constant temperature. If V is halved (V2 = V1/2), then P2 = 2P1. So the pressure doubles.

questions.text: A 60 W light bulb is used for 2 hours. How much electrical energy does it consume?
questions.explanation: Energy = Power x Time = 60 W x (2 x 3600 s) = 60 x 7200 = 432,000 J (432 kJ). Always convert time to seconds: 2 hours = 7200 s.
